title: Sweet and Sour Chicken

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: 500g (1 lb) boneless chicken breast or thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.title: For the Sauce
items: ¼ cup rice vinegar or white vinegar
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 3 tbsp water (slurry)
¼ cup pineapple juice (optional for authentic sweetness)

instruction.title: Prepare the Chicken
steps: In a bowl, whisk the egg.
In another bowl, mix cornstarch, flour, salt, and white pepper.
Dip chicken pieces in egg, then coat thoroughly with the flour mixture.
Heat oil in a deep pan to 170–180°C (340–360°F).
Fry chicken in batches until golden brown and cooked through (5–6 minutes). Drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Make the Sweet and Sour Sauce
steps: In a saucepan over medium heat, combine ketchup, vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, and pineapple juice.
Stir until sugar dissolves and sauce begins to simmer.
Slowly add cornstarch slurry, stirring constantly until sauce thickens. Remove from heat.

instruction.title: Stir-Fry Vegetables (Optional)
steps: Heat a little oil in a wok or large pan.
Quickly stir-fry onions and bell peppers for 2–3 minutes (they should remain slightly crisp).
Add pineapple chunks and stir-fry another minute.

instruction.title: Combine
steps: Toss fried chicken in the sweet and sour sauce until fully coated.
Add stir-fried vegetables and toss gently to combine.

instruction.title: Serve
steps: Serve immediately with steamed white rice or fried rice.
Garnish with sesame seeds or chopped green onions for extra flair.

chefTips: Use chicken thighs for juicier meat.
Fry chicken twice for extra crispiness.
Don’t overcook vegetables—they should remain vibrant and crunchy.
Balance sweetness and tanginess by adjusting sugar and vinegar to taste.
